entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg functionality not possible in Test Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are seeking an enhancement for the reordering or manual sequencing of Test Cases within a Test Plan in SAP Cloud ALM.  Some of our Test Plans have upwards of 50 Test Cases that must be completed in a specific sequence and the list of Test Cases must be shown in an order that we choose.

We’ve discovered on our own that we can manipulate the sequencing by manually and/or alphanumerically prefixing every Test Case title within the Test Plan, but we need a better solution than to repeatedly modify every Test Case title.

Does a different functionality already exists to sequence the Test Cases within the Test Plans?

 

Thanks,

 

PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg fun...
entry.content:
By default, the order test cases are assigned in a Test Plan is the order they are presented to the user.  The Test Plan application does offer a multi-level, user-controlled sort sequence based on Solution Process, Scope and/or Title -> check KBA 3610413 - How to re-order assigned Test Cases inside a Test Plan? - Cloud ALM

In case these offerings do not meet your sortation requirements, please feel free to log a request in the Influencer Campaign:

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release: What are the highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development?
entry.content:
Following the successful launch of SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development in late 2024, we are excited to unveil new innovations and enhancements designed to benefit both managers and employees.

With AI-assisted successor insights, managers can now evaluate and compare named successors for a position more easily. Leveraging generative AI capabilities, succession planners can gain insights into the successors that are nominated for a position. In the succession org chart or the position tile view, users can leverage this new feature by selecting the generate insights button in the header of a position card. The insights provide a comprehensive overall summary section which offers a high-level overview of the job role requirements and all nominated successors. It is divided into two main parts, the role overview and successors analysis. A detailed summary is provided for each successor, encompassing three key aspects including experience and progression, performance and key achievements, and strengths and potential areas for growth.

AI-assisted successor insights

Managers can also now track their team's career development in a summary view, including career aspirations and skill growth in the latest version of career worksheet. Managers can access the summary view via the growth portfolio team view, where they find a comprehensive overview of their team's roles, aspirations, and skills. The 'Roles and Aspirations' section displays current job roles and the top three most popular target roles, including team readiness and employee details. In the 'Identify Skill Strengths' section, managers can see the top five skills and attributes where employees excel, track skill development over time, and filter by roles or critical skills. Similarly, the 'Recognize Skills to Improve' section highlights the top five skills and attributes where employees have gaps, offering the same tracking and filtering functionalities to support targeted skill development. These insights make it easier to monitor and guide team development and identify priorities for growth at the team level.

Team development insights

Additionally, we introduced new enhancements to the opportunity marketplace feature. The vetting process has been improved for assignment owners and co-owners when reviewing applicants with the ability to pause an assignment posting. This gives them greater flexibility in managing existing applications. Also, employees can now identify and apply for assignments across multiple locations, while HR personnel and managers can view opportunity marketplace assignments within the latest people profile, ensuring greater visibility into assignment status.

To read about the latest 1H innovations for our other talent solutions check out:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning
  • SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals
  • Talent intelligence hub from SAP SuccessFactors

Explore more innovations across the suite in the 1H 2025 release by checking out the release highlights brochure, overview video featuring Bianka Woelke and much more on the Product Release & Road Map Information page.

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release: What are the highlights for the talent intelligence hub?
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce our enhanced role-based permissions for the talent intelligence hub. This update gives administrators the ability to select by specific attribute type or tag which attributes should be visible and ratable. The attributes in the growth portfolio can be restricted from the employee view based on the target criteria that is set for a target population. This enhancement helps administrators decide who can see which attributes and improves support for organizations with complex permissions requirements.

Additionally, with our new validation workflow for the growth portfolio, you can define an approval process to be triggered whenever a change is made to the rating of an attribute. From the growth portfolio, when there is a change to the proficiency level for which a workflow is configured, the request is sent to the designated reviewers for approval. The reviewers are notified and can approve or reject the request from the workflow to-do cards. From the approval request details page, the reviewers can take action to approve or reject. This validation process boosts governance and enhances awareness of growth portfolio activities.

Talent intelligence hub workflow support

For learners, we are offering skills-focused learning in the growth portfolio with a curated list of learning opportunities to improve key skills and competencies. Users can discover filtered learning options tailored to their upskilling needs under the new 'Advance My Proficiency' section. With this enhancement, employees can choose the right courses to improve their skills and advance in their careers.

Skills-focused learning opportunities

Interested to learn best practices for getting started or gaining traction with the talent intelligence hub? Just us on June 4th to gain a clear, step-by-step approach to adoption. Click here to register. 

To read about the latest 1H innovations for our talent solutions check out:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development
  • SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting
  • SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding
  • SAP SuccessFactors Learning
  • SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

entry.title: Re: Streamline asset inspection process with Visual Asset Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Hi @james_loar,

you are absolutely right, there is only a value from the image and the measurements taken from it if you can link this to the technical object (in the train example the thickness of the contact stripes).
This is why our visual inspection scenarios require to send the image with the business context, as you can see from our API documentation https://api.sap.com/api/VisualInspection_APIs/path/post_InspectionResult You must provide at least the technical object ID, the technical object type (equipment or FLOC), SSID and the visual inspection view (e.g. you have different camera views or inspection to be executed on the same technical object, this helps you to map it to a certain inspection view).
So yes, that is exactly how it is done in APM, maybe the screenshot in the video doesn't show the asset information as we maximized the image and visual inspection result, but the business context is always given.

To you last point: yes, you can do this with CBM rules. Simply create a monitoring rule, which checks for all trains if the last timestamp from the indicator value which is used to store the thickness/height is older then 7 days and create an alert, user notification or maintenance notification. You can schedule this rule to run every day and then you will see which trains might need to be manual inspected next time they come to a service point.
I hope your concerns are all addressed, if not don't be shy an reach out to me directly.

BR
Philipp

entry.title: Orlando Wrapped, Madrid Awaits: Your SAP Sapphire 2025 Utilities Guide
entry.content:
The energy in Orlando was electric as SAP Sapphire 2025 kicked off and showcased how it's transforming enterprise operations through SAP Business AI, integrating applications, data, and AI to help businesses thrive amid uncertainty. Key innovations include new AI agents and the widespread availability of the AI co-pilot Joule, aimed at boosting user productivity by 30% and enabling faster, more confident decision-making.

For our sector, the utility-focused sessions were particularly inspiring. We extend our gratitude to our customers, including Loudoun Water, Vistra, SCE, NPPD, NextEra, Southwest Gas, People's Gas, and others, for sharing their invaluable project insights, outcomes, and experiences. These industry pioneers are shaping the future of energy and water, and we are privileged to support their transformation journeys.

On May 20th, Southern California Edison (SCE) showcased how utility companies can enhance customer experience through SAP Business Suite and SAP Signavio. In their presentation, Karen Cheung (SCE) and Jay Castleburry (SAP) emphasized the importance of strategic planning and demonstrated how Signavio helps uncover hidden opportunities—enabling small investments to yield significant returns. Their story stands as a compelling example of business-driven transformation.

 

 

Another standout attraction was the Learning & Certification Lab—a dynamic space where participants could test their skills through lightning-fast quizzes and experience how gamified learning transforms curiosity into real growth. It was a vivid reminder that learning is a continuous journey—and it’s even better when we grow together. #SkillUpwithSAP

Last but not least, Innovation is the driving force behind progress, and this was powerfully demonstrated through the inspiring and immersive industry showcases.

You missed Sapphire Orlando: Check the recordings on SAP Sapphire virtual.

entry.title: Re: Create Variable for 0HIER_VERS
entry.content: In Eclipse, you create a variable on 0HIER_VERS.  The challenge I faced was that despite being assigned to the 0BWTCT_ESS infoArea in the RSDIOBJ table, it is not visible in the infoArea hierarchy.  The workaround I found is you right click on any infoObject, choose NEW>VARIABLE and then in the pop-up where you enter the variable name, you can also change the Ref. Characteristic from whatever IOBJ you right clicked on to 0HIER_VERS.

entry.title: Re: How to obtain Packing Time Stamp on Outbound Delivery...
entry.content:
Hi @SAP_Talks, Is there any way to pick this time stamp and place it at the Packing Status DPC ? -> Unfortunately Status DPC is transient ( values fill during runtime). 

"and also is it possible to show Packing status as "completed" if the Pack HU is created" -> It depends with your business processes, if you are using posc and pack step as completion where you can fetch info from that "HU created and completely packed" however if you have 2 layer packing example building carton (1 level Packing) then building Pallet ( 2 nd Level Packing)... depends on business processes.

In my opinion if your business is looking for details with full of information related for precise reporting, go for additional fields and fill values with enhancement in monitor rather than enhancement with Status DPC.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Inquiry on Re-uploading Opening Balances
entry.content:
Hello all,

We would like to clarify a technical possibility regarding our SAP BusinessbyDesign environment.

Background:

  • Our cutover date was set as 1 September 2024, and we migrated opening balances as of 31 August 2024.

  • Since then, the system has been live, and the team has been actively creating purchase requisitions, purchase orders, and supplier invoices, along with corresponding postings across modules (MM, FI, etc.).

Question:
Is it technically possible to remove or reverse all existing transactional data (since 1 September 2024) and re-upload a revised opening balance as of 30 April 2025?

There is no monthly closing been done, only open transactions in the system.

If yes:

  • What would be the scope and complexity of such an operation?

  • What are the potential risks, side effects, or system limitations we should be aware of?

  • Would such an action compromise data integrity, audit trails, or system consistency?

  • Are there any recommended SAP-supported procedures or tools to handle such a rollback?

We understand this is a non-standard scenario and would greatly appreciate your guidance on whether this is technically feasible and advisable.

If there is any document, please send it to my personal email id.

Thanks in advance.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Hey , Please can we delete a chart of account in SAP...
entry.content:
Chart of Accounts in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it is not possible to delete the standard chart of accounts (YCOA) or create a completely new one [1]. The system comes with a pre-delivered and assigned operating chart of accounts (YCOA) that cannot be changed fundamentally . However, you can adapt the standard chart of accounts to meet your needs in the following ways:

  1. Renumber/rename existing G/L accounts in the YCOA chart of accounts [1].

  2. Add new G/L accounts to the YCOA chart of accounts [1].

  3. Map your existing G/L accounts to the standard accounts in the delivered chart of accounts during implementation [1].

  4. Use an Alternative Chart of Accounts for local purposes .

Key points to note:

  • The standard YCOA cannot be deleted or replaced with a custom chart of accounts [1].

  • Deletion is not supported for the "Edit Chart of Accounts List" (SSCUI 102422) functionality .

  • The system does not allow assigning a custom operating chart of accounts, unlike in on-premise versions .

  • For specific industries or countries/regions, you may be able to map standard G/L account numbers and descriptions to industry-specific or country-specific ones using variant operating charts of accounts [2].

To adapt the standard chart of accounts during implementation:

  1. Prepare the mapping of your G/L accounts to the standard accounts in the Explore Phase [1].

  2. Involve an experienced Finance consultant early in the process [1].

  3. Implement changes using configuration activities in your configuration environment [1].

  4. Consider account ID length adjustments if needed [1].

If you need further customization beyond what's possible with YCOA, it's recommended to use the Alternative Chart of Accounts for your specific local requirements .

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Which notification to use when – Workflow, Situation Handling, KPIs, etc
entry.content:
TL;DR – Know when to use which SAP Fiori notifications.  That is, workflow versus situation handling versus Smart Business KPI subscriptions versus process-specific notifications, and which technique to use if you need to create clean core custom notifications.  

A very common business requirement is to notify someone that something has happened and, if possible, get them started on responding to it. 

  • In classic SAP ECC you would use typically workflow notifications and/or email notifications. Unfortunately, over time this often led to a flood of emails, emails being ignored, and increasingly poor response times.
  • In SAP S/4HANA there are a range of more useful, more nuanced, notification techniques available in SAP Fiori that encourage prompt action. You can optionally send them as push notifications to your tablet or phone. These notifications don’t flood your email inbox, however if you want email notifications, you can optionally echo selected notifications to email.

These notifications are deliberately difficult to miss! You will find your notifications in a variety of places, including the notification center of the SAP Fiori launchpad as shown in the example below: 

Why this blog series?  During workshops with one my recent customers currently in transition to SAP S/4HANA, we came across a point of confusion. They are very interested in using Situation Handling but were getting confused about when to use Situation Handling versus Workflow versus some other notification technique. In this blog you will find the different notification techniques explained and when to use them. 

There is a quick summary of the SAP Fiori notification techniques discussed in this blog post in the image below. These techniques are also listed in the table later in this blog post in section SAP Notification Techniques in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them.

In this first blog post, you will learn:

  • Part 1 - SAP Fiori notification techniques available in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them; when to use email notifications to complement or supplement SAP Fiori notifications. 

Later in this blog series you will learn

  • Part 2 - The impact on business users: i.e. where notifications are visible in the SAP Fiori launchpad of SAP S/4HANA and elsewhere such as: SAP Fiori app F0862 My Inbox, SAP Fiori app F6511 My Outbox, SAP Fiori app F4154 My Situations, SAP Fiori app F4537 My Situations - Extended, SAP Build Work Zone,  SAP Task Center, SAP Mobile Start, and elsewhere.

You will also look under the covers at what your team needs to do to enable notifications.

  • Part 3 - For Key Users, Functional Consultants and UX Architects: Where & how to configure each notification technique, and FAQ.
  • Part 4 - For Administrators and Developers, understand prerequisites such as the technical notification channel and notification provider that drives predefined and custom notification techniques, what's involved in setting up predefined notifications versus custom notifications, and some Troubleshooting tips.

Further references: 

  • For the difference between classic workflows, flexible workflows, and related cloud workflow techniques in SAP BTP refer to SAP Help - ABAP Platform - Workflow
  • For the difference between workflow and situations, and the difference between standard vs extended situations, refer to SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA – Process Management and Integration – Situation Handling - FAQ.
  • For understanding Smart Business KPIs refer to SAP Help - SAP S/4HANA - Analytics - Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
  • Although much has changed, there is still some useful background information in our earlier blog post SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Notification Center - Part 1 - Activation 

 

SAP Notification Techniques in SAP S/4HANA and when to use them

In the table below, you will find the 4 main SAP Fiori notification techniques to choose from based on your use case.

Use Case

Example

Notification Technique

Configured By

Notify an approver they have an approvals task

Notify approver of a new Purchase Order requiring their approval

Workflow notifications - flexible workflow (preferred) or classic workflow (supported)

Key User or UX Architect

Notify a business expert of a business situation to be resolved

 

Notify someone that: a contract is about to expire; a batch job has failed with a business-critical error, etc.

Situation Handling notifications - standard or extended

Key User or UX Architect

Notify that a KPI will soon be/has been exceeded target threshold

Notify the responsible manager that actual budget has reached 90% of planned budget

Smart Business KPI notifications

Key User or Analytics Specialist

Notify someone that a process has completed successfully

Notify Purchaser of the number of PRs automatically converted to POs today

Process-specific notifications

Administrator 

When choosing between these notification types the main factors to consider are:

  • Traceability – Where you have very strict highly-controlled processes – such as approvals (also known as delegations of authority) - workflow notifications provide end-to-end traceability of every step of the process. You can find the status of the approval at any time and have an audit trail history (via the workflow log) of what has occurred including who was involved in each task.
  • Productivity – When working with business experts, you want them to know a business situation has happened and to be supported in how to resolve the business situation.  However, you don’t want the business expert to feel micro-managed as that could make them less productive and even impact their mental health!  Situation handling is the right choice here to aid swift resolution while showing that you trust the business expert to take the appropriate action. You can find the status of the situation’s business object at any time, which situation type was triggered, and which team was involved. The user acting on the business object is deliberately not tracked by the situation, however if they have made a change to a business object that will be tracked, as usual, in the change log history of the relevant business object.
  • Expected Response –
    • Workflow notifications require a strict response – for example, the approver must approve or reject a purchase order. 
    • Situation notifications require a flexible response, the business expert must decide what is the appropriate action to take to resolve the situation.
    • Most other notifications – both Smart Business KPIs and process-specific notifications – are primarily about keeping people informed with no specific response needed.
  • Notification source – To make sure the recipient is notified as quickly as possible, it makes sense to trigger the notification from the workflow, situation, KPI or process that first identifies the need for the notification. 
    • Often there are predefined notification providers that provide easy options to configure & trigger these notifications.  
    • Where you don’t have a predefined notification provider, creating a custom notification provider is a relatively easy task for a developer, and worth considering.

 

Understanding the Effort required to Configure Notifications

Skills needed for configuring all predefined notification use cases are LOW. 

Creating your own custom notifications is a LOW to MEDIUM effort for a Developer with a small amount of configuration required by an Administrator.

 

When to use email notifications instead of SAP Fiori notifications

There are 2 additional notification use cases that commonly occur. While they may seem like separate notification types, they are essentially just variations of the notification types listed above. In these cases, an email notification is more likely to be required. These also are LOW effort to configure. 

 

Use Case

Example

How to Handle

Configured By

Notify a casual employee that something has occurred

Notify a user who doesn’t login very often

 

User needs to login at least to the SAP Fiori launchpad. They can go to their personal Settings & opt to echo notifications to email. That is, in the User Actions > Settings > Notifications area. They can only configure notification types they have received at least once.

Business User

Notify an external person that something has occurred

 

Notify a supplier that a quote has been accepted

 

In this situation, the external person does not have access to the SAP Fiori launchpad.  Where needed, notifications to external suppliers are typically included in process-specific notifications. 

Key User or Functional Consultant

 

Sending aggregated email notifications of SAP Fiori notifications

For notifications where a response is expected, you may want to notify the user by multiple channels.

For workflow notifications the Workflow Extended Notifications (program SWN_SELSEN) is still available and can be scheduled as a batch program. You configure workflow extended notifications transactions SWN_CONFIG and SWNADMIN. You can include a link to SAP Fiori app F0862 My Inbox in the email.

Hint: To minimize searching through emails for past notifications, you should consider adding app F6511 My Outbox to all users who have My Inbox. My Outbox enables users to view the workflow log and task log of workflows they have been involved in, allowing them to see what happened before/after their involvement.

For situations, in app F2947 Manage Situation Types or F5437 Manage Situation Types – Extended, you can configure the option to send an email for aggregated notifications.

Refer to

  • SAP Help – ABAP Platform – SAP Business Workflow Reference Documentation - Extended Notifications for SAP Business Workflow
  • SAP Note 2892592 - WF Notifications: Generate link to My Inbox
  • SAP Note 3589593 - Setting Up Workflow Extended Notification Mail for Specific Multiple Recipients
  • SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA – Process Management and Integration - Situation Handling - Notifications

 

Notification techniques to avoid

Avoid using classic SAP Business Workflow “Display Notification” Tasks  - i.e. Task Type = Notification. These classic notifications are only visible in the classic SAP Office Inbox and have very limited features.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Why at the time of goods receipt in MIGO on the Quantity tab QTY in SUK coming wrong
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I have a question regarding goods receipt using movement type 109 (Origin Accept). On the "Quantity" tab, the Quantity in Unit of Entry is shown as 10.667 CV, and the Quantity in SUK is also 10.667 LB.

Here are the relevant unit definitions:

  • CV = Case (Alternative Unit)

  • LB = Pound (Base Unit of Measure)

On the "Units of Measure (Additional)" tab, the conversion is defined as:

1,000 CV = 9,375 LB,
or
1 CV = 9.375 LB

Given this conversion, 10.667 CV should equal approximately 100 LB, not 10.667 LB.

So my question is:
Why is the "Quantity in SUK" showing 10.667 LB instead of the expected ~100 LB? Is the system calculating it incorrectly, or is there something misconfigured in the unit of measure setup?

Please help clarify where this discrepancy might be coming from.

See attached images.

 

 Thanks and Regards

kashif.

entry.title: SAP 分析云建模扩展与集成 2025 年 QRC2 版本更新内容
entry.content:
目的

亲爱的SAP社区同事们：

在2025年QRC2版本中，SAP 分析云的“建模”中推出了一系列令人兴奋的新功能，现已正式上线！

概览

了解更多新功能详情：

  1. 用户管理的单位换算
  2. 货币汇率表迁移到文件资源库
  3. 数据导入API——允许导入和API访问切换

1. 用户管理的单位换算

标准单位换算功能已得到增强，允许用户在“标准单位表”中创建、编辑或删除单位和单位组。

新的“单位说明表”使得单位在SAP 分析云的其他功能（如图表和表格）中能够以更有意义的方式显示。该表还支持多达40种语言的单位语言转换。请参阅SAP Note 3531711 - SAP 分析云中标准单位换算支持的语言列表，了解其所支持的语言。

“标准单位表”和“单位说明表”可以通过建模器的“换算”选项卡访问。

扩展的数据导出和数据导入 API 支持此自定义单位功能，以便于更新后端中的单位表。

自定义单位也可以由拥有 Datasphere 工作区的 SAC 建模人员和计划人员维护。

查看我们新的标准单位换算教程，了解如何在 SAP 分析云中创建单位换算度量。

单位换算教程

要了解在SAP 分析云中设置和创建单位换算的完整工作流程，请参考Amanda的“在SAP 分析云中使用单位换算”博客。

2. 货币汇率表迁移到文件资源库

货币汇率表已集成到 SAP 分析云的文件资源库区域。

以下部分概述了在使用货币汇率表时可以看到的更改的位置。

货币与我的文件

现在可以创建并将货币汇率表保存到文件资源库中，这些文件夹可以在“我的文件”中看到。

在这里，也可以根据需要选择、移动、更改授权或删除货币汇率表。

从“我的文件”导航到“货币换算”

现有的货币汇率表可以在“文件/我的文件/公用/ Currency Conversions”文件夹中找到。

迁移后的货币汇率表文件夹

货币与换算选项卡

在“建模器”的“换算”选项卡中，列出了最近使用的货币汇率表及其文件夹结构位置。

保存新创建的表时，会提供一个导航面板，供您选择要将货币汇率表保存到何处。

这包括为具有相关权限的用户保存到 Datasphere 中的工作区。

货币和导入数据/计划作业

您仍然可以使用“导入”按钮将货币数据从文件或远程数据源导入到汇率表中。

可以从远程数据源（例如从 BPC、Google Drive、HANA 或 BW）导入作业，并且可以在货币汇率表的数据管理工作区中进行查看和计划。

可以从下拉菜单中选择一个汇率表，该菜单包括其目录结构或Datasphere工作区位置。

从首选位置选择货币汇率表

货币与 BPC

BPC 的工作流程已更改。已移除“使用BPC创建货币汇率表”选项。用户应创建并保存其货币汇率表，然后使用导入按钮从 BPC 导入数据。BPC现在与其他货币数据源工作流流程相同。

模型中的货币

在模型的“模型首选项”部分中，在“货币换算”设置中选择货币汇率表时，会显示货币汇率表名称及其目录结构位置。

货币换算度量的创建保持不变。

从模型首选项选择货币汇率表

货币与安全

在“安全/角色”中，货币不再显示一个下拉箭头来列出所有货币汇率表。可以通过角色为所有汇率表分配读取权限。

在“安全/团队”中，作为迁移过程的一部分，新的汇率角色（RateRoles）已自动创建。根据用户在其租户上分配的现有角色，用户将自动包含在这些角色中。例如，具有BI_Content_Creator角色的用户将自动分配到RateRole_BI_Content_Creator团队。

可以进一步从“我的文件”管理个别汇率表的权限。

货币与传输

在SAP 分析云的“传输”部分，用户现在可以在导出和导入功能的公用文件夹中查看货币汇率表。

用户也可以单独导出或导入这些表，为用户提供了更多的灵活性。

货币换算性能提升

在将源货币单位换算为目标货币单位时，性能也得到了改进，2025年还将计划进一步优化。

货币汇率表与Datasphere

我们继续投资 SAP Datasphere 无缝集成计划。新创建的货币汇率表可以保存到Datasphere工作区中建模者或计划人员偏好的文件夹中。

当具有Datasphere访问权限的SAP 分析云用户遇到Datasphere工作区被锁定时，将无法更新该工作区内的任何货币汇率表，直到工作区解锁。

3. 数据导入API——允许通过API进行导入

迄今为止，数据导入API可以通过2-legged（机器对机器）或3-legged（授权用户）连接将数据导入任何模型，没有任何限制。

通过在模型中引入“支持通过数据导入 API 进行导入”复选框，现在可以控制在使用数据导入API时是否可以将数据导入到特定模型。这种控制也可以应用于存储在Datasphere工作区环境中的SAP分析云模型。

在模型首选项“访问权限和隐私”部分中，现在有一个名为“支持通过数据导入 API 进行导入”的新选项。

选中复选框以允许通过数据导入API导入数据

当复选框被选中时，允许通过数据导入API导入数据；当复选框未被选中时，限制导入。

如果用户在未选中API选项的情况下尝试执行导入操作，将返回一条提示消息，提示该模型被限制导入。

结论

总结来说，作为2025年QRC2版本的一部分，SAP 分析云的建模扩展与集成领域引入了以下新功能和更新。

  1. 随着“用户管理”的单位换算功能的推出，客户可以在“标准单位表”和“单位说明表”中管理自己的自定义单位和单位组。支持多达40种语言的单位翻译。对于使用Datasphere工作区的客户，也提供了单位处理的支持。
  2. 客户可以管理其货币汇率表的首选项和授权，包括保存到 Datasphere 工作区。迁移后的表位于/我的文件/公用/ Currency Conversions。
  3. 现在，客户可以通过在模型偏好设置中选择“支持通过数据导入 API 进行导入”选项，来管理特定模型（包括存储在Datasphere工作区中的模型）的数据导入。

如果您有任何疑问，请随时在下方留言或在我们的SAP 分析云问答论坛中提问。

如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期（QRC）发布的信息，请查看这篇SAP知识库文章。

如需进一步了解，请访问我们的SAP 分析云社区页面，以获取更多产品信息、最佳实践等内容。

关于QRC发布日期的更多信息，请参阅2025年SAP分析云季度发布周期（QRC）更新。

查看我们的SAP产品路线图，以了解SAP 分析云的更多即将推出的功能。

感谢您的阅读！

 

原文作者：Amanda

原文链接：https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-analytics-cloud-modeling-extensions-amp-integration-qrc2/ba-p/14028056

entry.title: Why TextPool is Better Than Script Variables in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), you've probably used script variables to manage global values in your stories or applications. But there’s a new object in town called TextPool, and it might just make your life easier in certain situations.

Let’s break down what TextPool is, how it's different from script variables, and when you should consider using it.

What is TextPool in SAP Analytics Cloud?

TextPool is a relatively new scripting object in SAC that allows you to store and manage string values centrally in your stories or analytic applications. Think of it as a global string manager — you define it once and use it wherever you need it.

You might be thinking, “We already have script variables for that.” And yes, script variables can also store values globally. But TextPool is designed specifically for constant strings — meaning values that shouldn’t change once defined.

Why Should You Use TextPool Instead of Script Variables?

Here are a few reasons why TextPool can be a better choice in specific scenarios:

✅1. Constant String Protection

With script variables, even after setting a value, there’s always a risk of accidentally updating it somewhere in the story. TextPool avoids that because the values are not meant to be changed dynamically.

✅2. Cleaner Reusability Across Objects

Let’s say you’re working on an API-based project and you need to reuse your API key across multiple widgets — buttons, input fields, etc.
With script variables, you’d have to initialize the value during onInitialization, and the value could still potentially change.
But with TextPool, you define your string once and use it as-is anywhere in your story.

✅3. Faster Access and Less Overhead

TextPool values load faster compared to variables initialized at runtime. This is helpful especially in large-scale applications with many dependencies.

✅4. Grouping Values Like an Array

You can add multiple text entries in a single TextPool — sort of like storing key-value pairs. This helps keep things organized and avoids confusion when you’re managing many string constants.

Example Use Case

Imagine you’re building an app where you don’t want certain strings like "API_KEY" or "DEFAULT_ERROR_MESSAGE" to change accidentally. Using a script variable may lead to someone overwriting them later. But with TextPool, once you’ve defined them, they’re safe — no accidental updates.

How to Create a TextPool in SAC

Creating a TextPool is simple:

  1. Open your Story in SAP Analytics Cloud.

  2. Go to the Outline tab.

  3. Under the Scripting section, click the plus (+) icon.

  4. Select TextPool.

  5. Give your TextPool a name.

  6. Add text entries by clicking the plus (+) icon inside the TextPool panel.

You can add multiple key-value pairs like:

  • "api_key" = "1234-xyz"

  • "error_msg" = "Something went wrong!"

How to Use TextPool in Your Scripts

To retrieve a value from TextPool, use the getText() method like this:

This always returns a string.

  • If you need it in another format (like a number or boolean), you can cast it manually after retrieving it.

Final Thoughts

TextPool may seem like a small feature, but it’s incredibly handy in large applications or when you want to store static string values safely and efficiently. While script variables still have their place, using TextPool gives you that extra control and clarity in your code.

I personally use TextPool whenever I’m working on a big project and want my constants to be uneditable, consistent, and easily reusable.

entry.title: Re: Create Freight Settlement Document Button Shows an Empty Page
entry.content:
Hello Karthikeyan,

Was the issue resolved for you? I am facing a similar issue. Could you please guide me how this was resolved? I am bit new to SAP TM module.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Free Search and list sort in Fiori Application

change to lower value to see if it makes any difference

I don't think the SAP Enables Now support team will do this, especially now, provided that SAP Enable Now is in a maintenance mode. 

Anyway, you can ask them. Submit a ticket for the SEN-KM-MGR component.

entry.title: SAP Business AI × BTP 勉強会 2025 参加レポート！AI活用の「知る」から「使う」 そしてさらに深く「知る」へ
entry.content:
先日、JSUG（ジャパンSAPユーザーグループ）が主催する若手コミュニティ「Next-Gen Boost」の企画で、「SAP Business AI × BTP 勉強会 2025」に参加する機会を得ました。すこしだけ長くIT業界に身を置く私にとって、若い世代の熱気と最新技術への真摯な取り組みに触れることは、まさに「Boost」そのものでした。特にSAP BTPの経験が浅い参加者を対象としつつも、SAPが今後注力するBusiness AIを体系的に理解し、体験できる内容構成はよく練られており、非常に活気に満ちた学びの場でした。今回は、その体験を少し振り返ってみたいと思います。

 

 


1. イベント概要：体系的な学びの設計

この勉強会は、4月14日、21日、28日の3回にわたる連続開催でした。開催目的は「特にSAP BTPの経験がない方々が、SAPの注力領域であるSAP Business AIをスムーズに理解し、実際に体験できる構成となっています。」という通り、初日は座学でAI技術の概要を学び、続く2回でハンズオンを通じて体感するという流れです。座学で得た知識を、実機操作を通して定着させるという構成は、非常に理にかなっており、学習効果を高める上で有効だと感じました。

 

 

2. 座学セッション：SAP Business AIの全体像を掴む

初日（4月14日）は、SAP Business AIの全体像と具体的なユースケースの把握が主眼でした。セッションでは「SAPのAIって結局何ができるの？」という、誰もが抱くであろう素朴な疑問に対し、実際の活用事例を交えながら、その特徴や活用領域が分かりやすく紹介されました。
SAP Business AIの全体像とコンポーネント構成がコンパクトにまとまっており、各AIユースケースにおいて、どのコンポーネントがどのように連携しているのかが丁寧に説明されたことで、深い理解へと繋がりました。何より印象的だったのは、講演者の方々がご自身の言葉で、腹落ちするまで説明してくださった点です。紋切り型の説明ではなく、自らの理解に基づいた解説は、聴衆に安心感と親しみやすさを与え、学びをより深いものにしてくれたと感じています。

 

 

3. ハンズオンセッション：肌で感じるAIとBTPの可能性

第2回（4月21日）と第3回（4月28日）は、いよいよハンズオン形式です。AIサービスやBTPの活用方法を実際に操作しながら学ぶ貴重な機会となりました。参加者は、事前に用意された3つのチュートリアルの中から興味のあるテーマを選んで取り組みました。提供されたハンズオンシナリオは、「SAP BTP Trial Workbooks: Generative AI and Development」の一部であり、以下の3つです。

  • シナリオ1：SAP Generative AI Hub with AI Launchpad, Orchestration and Document Grounding
    Generative AI HubのAI Launchpadの紹介から、様々なプロバイダーの大規模言語モデル（LLM）をビジネスに組み込む方法、LLMプロンプトの作成・管理、そしてDocument Groundingを用いたRAG検索の実践が含まれていました。
  • シナリオ2：SAP HANA Cloud with Vector Engine and GenAI Hub
     SAP Generative AI HubをLangChainフレームワーク経由で公開し、SAP HANA Cloudのベクトル埋め込みと組み合わせる方法を体験する内容です。
  •  シナリオ3：SAP Build Code – Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot
     Joule copilotを活用したGenerative AIベースの開発で、SAP Build CodeでCustomer Loyalty Managementアプリケーションを作成、モデル化、実行する内容でした。SAP Build Code、SAP S/4HANA Cloud、SAP Build Apps間の統合機能も紹介されました。データエンティティやバックエンドロジックをSAP Build CodeとJoule AI copilotで作成し、UIアプリケーションをSAP Build Appsで作成してデータを消費するという、実践的なビジネスシナリオでした。

ハンズオン環境は各自PC持ち込みでしたが、運営側がスムーズに接続できる環境を準備してくださり、ストレスなく取り組めました。操作に戸惑う場面もありましたが、参加者同士で助け合ったり、講師陣に積極的に質問したりと、少人数ならではの対話の多さが魅力でした。互いに知見を共有し、協力し合う姿は、多くの刺激となりました。

 

 

4. ハンズオンを終えて：実践を通して見えてくる課題と発見

私はハンズオン初日（4月21日）にシナリオ1に挑戦しました。提供されたワークブックに従い、設定作業は目安時間内におおむね完了しましたが、時間の制約もあり、正直なところ「いったい何をしていたのか」をじっくりと咀嚼する時間は取れませんでした。ワークブックは「こうすれば」「こうなる」という形で問題と解答が示されているため、理論上は読み進めるだけでもハンズオンの意図を汲み取れますが、実際に理解を深めるには、一旦動作するものを構築した後、少しずつ設定を変えて試行錯誤することが肝要だと感じました。また、座学で学んだことと設定した部分を照らし合わせ、全体の中でどの部分を設定したのかを俯瞰的に確認することも、効果的な復習方法だと再認識しました。
ハンズオンはワークブックがあれば一人でも進められるようになっていますが、特にBTPのように進化の速いツールでは、UIなどがワークブックと異なるケースも少なくありません。そんな時、つまずいた際に質問できる人が近くにいるかいないかは、非常に大きいと感じました。複数人で取り組むことで、問題解決が格段に早まることを実感しました。
ハンズオン2日目（4月28日）までには、仕事の合間に残りの2つのシナリオを予習しました。シナリオ2は所要時間が最短でしたが、設定値が見当たらず途中で断念。最も所要時間が長かったシナリオ3は、実際に動作するアプリケーションが完成するため、イメージがしやすかったです。このシナリオはアプリケーションの動作自体にAIが関連するのではなく、Joule copilotがアプリケーション作成を支援してくれるというものでした。プロンプトを与えることでアプリケーションの一部が自動生成されるのですが、ワークブックのコピペで予定調和的に完成していくため、実際の業務ではどの程度の揺らぎを許容するのかなど、様々なパターンを試してみる必要があると感じました。実務では、質の高いプロンプト集の準備が必須になるだろう、という示唆を得られました。
2日目のハンズオンは予習の甲斐もあり、少し余裕を持って臨めました。予習でうまくいかなかったシナリオ2は、別の問題が発生しているという情報もあり断念。残りの時間はシナリオ3のデバッグに費やしました。アプリの特定の画面機能が動作しないという問題が発生し、サポートとして参加されていたSAPの社員さんに助けを求めました。おかげで故障箇所の見当をつけていただき、改修に至るヒントを得ることができました。ワークショップの時間内には問題解決には至りませんでしたが、糸口を見つけられただけでも大きな収穫でした。

 

 


5. 総括：学びと繋がりのある「放課後の同好会」

今回の勉強会は、19時からという業務時間後でも参加しやすい時間設定でした。JSUG会員企業の社員であれば無料で参加でき、軽食やお弁当まで提供される手厚さ。基本的な座学から、実際に仕組みを作り上げるハンズオンまでがセットになった「オールインワン」で学べる機会だと感じています。困ったことがあればサポートメンバーが丁寧に教えてくれる（あるいは一緒に考えてくれる）環境も非常に良かったです。
少人数制のため講師との距離も近く、受講者同士で活発な情報交換ができたのも大きな魅力でした。参加者は若手中心でしたが、中堅からベテランまで、誰もが参加しやすい雰囲気でした。（運営者の皆さん、細やかなお気遣いをありがとうございました。）
学びと新たな繋がり、その両方が得られる、非常に質の高い勉強会だったと心から感じています。まさに、堅苦しさのない「放課後の同好会」のような雰囲気で、楽しく新しい技術を学ぶには最適の場でした。

 

 

6. この記事を読む方へのメッセージ：一歩踏み出す勇気を

SAP Business AIやBTPに興味はあるものの、「何から始めて良いか分からない」「一人で学ぶのは難しそう」と感じている方もいらっしゃるかもしれません。また、新しい技術が日々進化する一方で、「実務の中で新しい技術に触れる機会がない」「仕事が忙しくて研修を受ける時間が取れない」「しばらく技術に触れていないな（私自身）」といった悩みを抱えている方も少なくないでしょう。
今回の勉強会のように、全体像の解説から実践的なハンズオンまでがセットになった形式は、効率よく体系的に学びを深める上で非常に有効だと実感しました。さらに、講師や他の参加者と直接交流できる場があることで、疑問点をその場で解消したり、新たな視点を得たりできる点は、独学では決して得られない大きなメリットです。
SAPの最新技術に関心がある方は、今後もし同様の勉強会やハンズオンの機会があれば、ぜひ参加を検討してみてはいかがでしょうか。それはきっと、学びの「知る」から「使う」へ、そして「さらに深く知る」へと一歩踏み出す、素晴らしいきっかけとなるはずです。

最後になりますが、この勉強会を主催・運営、そしていろいろ教えてくださった皆さんに感謝を申し上げます。本当に、ありがとうございました。

 

 


【おまけ】日本語プロンプトの可能性を探る

記事本編では技術的な詳細にはあまり触れませんでしたが、個人的に日本語プロンプトがどの程度受け入れられるかを試した結果について、簡単に整理しておきます。
シナリオ3では、ワークブックに用意されたプロンプト（英語）を使って、データエンティティ、テストデータ、バックグラウンドロジックを作成するシナリオが含まれていました。そこで、このワークブックをブラウザの翻訳機能で日本語に翻訳し、翻訳された日本語プロンプトを使って、同様のアプリケーション開発（バックエンド処理側：SAP Build Code）ができるかどうかを検証してみました。

 


日本語プロンプトによるバックグラウンドロジックの置き換え

まず、基本的なポイント計算ロジックを指示するために、ワークブックでは以下の英語プロンプトが使用されていました。

使用した英語プロンプト:
Reward points of each purchase will be the one-tenth of the purchase value. Each purchase value will be added to the total purchase value of the related customer. Each reward points will be added to the total reward points of the related customer.

これにより、「購入金額の10分の1」をポイントとして計算するロジックを含むコードが生成されました。（これはワークブック通りの手順なので当然の結果です。）


次に、購入金額に応じたポイント計算のルール変更を指示するため、条件分岐ロジックを日本語プロンプトで追加してみました。元の英語プロンプトをブラウザ翻訳した日本語をベースに、計算ロジックの肝となる部分のみを日本語で具体的に記述しました。

日本語プロンプト原文（英語プロンプトのブラウザによる日本語訳）:
ご購入ごとに付与されるポイントは、ご購入金額の10分の1となります。ご購入金額は、対象となるお客様の合計ご購入金額に加算されます。また、付与されるポイントは、対象となるお客様の合計ポイントに加算されます。

 

使用した日本語プロンプト（ロジックの条件分岐を手動で追加）:
ご購入ごとに付与されるポイントは、ご購入金額が10000を超える場合は20分の1、それ以外10分の1はとなります。ご購入金額は、対象となるお客様の合計ご購入金額に加算されます。また、付与されるポイントは、対象となるお客様の合計ポイントに加算されます。


この日本語プロンプトをJouleに与えた結果、以下のように条件分岐を含むロジックが正しく生成されました。


生成されたコード（関連部分）:
// Calculate reward points based on purchase value
const rewardPoints = purchaseData.purchaseValue > 10000
? Math.floor(purchaseData.purchaseValue / 20)
: Math.floor(purchaseData.purchaseValue / 10);
purchaseData.rewardPoints = rewardPoints;

このコードは、「ご購入金額が10000を超える場合は20分の1、それ以外は10分の1」という日本語での指示通りのロジックを正確に反映しています。


これらの事例から、SAP Jouleは日本語で記述されたプロンプトであっても、ロジックの追加や変更といった指示を適切に解釈し、意図通りのコードを生成できたことが分かります。

 

日本語プロンプトによるデータエンティティの作成

一方で、データエンティティ作成についても、手順自体はおおむね良好に進み、想定したものができたようでした。しかし、データエンティティ作成時の項目名定義において、英語から日本語への翻訳（ブラウザ）と日本語から英語への翻訳（Joule）の部分で翻訳の「揺らぎ」が発生し、結果として後続の処理が動作しないという結果となりました。

  • 英語原文プロンプト→Jouleによるコード生成
    原文プロンプト： Purchase Value
    → 作成された項目名： totalPurchaseValue

  • 英語原文プロンプト→ブラウザで翻訳した日本語プロンプト→Jouleによるコード生成
    原文プロンプト： Purchase Value
    → ブラウザが翻訳した日本語プロンプト： 購入金額
    　→ 作成された項目名： totalPurchaseAmount


今回発生した問題はAIというより、言語自身（同義異音）の問題のような気がしますが、留意点であることには変わりはありません。


今回の検証だけで、日本語プロンプトが実務で「完全に使える」という結論を出すには至りませんが、一定のコツや癖を理解しながら慣れていくことで、使える範囲が広がってくるような印象を持ちました。このようなことを実際に試せるのも、ハンズオンの大きなメリットだと改めて感じました。

この記事が、SAPの最新技術に関心を持つ皆さんの、新たな一歩を後押しするきっかけになれば幸いです。なお、あくまで個人的興味に基づく、簡単な検証ですので参考程度にしていただければと思います。

entry.title: Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation
entry.content:
Efficiently managing user access and roles is crucial for maintaining security and streamlining operations. By controlling authorization to Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM) from SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS), organizations can centralize user management, automate role assignments, and ensure seamless access control. This approach enhances security and simplifies administrative tasks, making it easier for administrators to manage user permissions across various applications.

This blog covers two options for implementing role mapping between CIS and AEM:

  1. Using CIS Groups

  2. Using a Federated Identity Provider (e.g., Auth0, Microsoft Entra Id, etc..)

 

Option 1 - Using SAP Cloud Identity Services Groups

Prerequisites

  • You have administrator access to AEM and the SAP CIS instance associated with that AEM application.

Configure Cloud Identity Services

  1. Log in to the CIS Console and navigate to the Groups screen.

  2. Create groups for the roles you’d like to map to (You can view the different AEM roles here - https://help.pubsub.em.services.cloud.sap/Cloud/cloud-user-management.htm#roles-and-permissions.)

  3. Ensure you have at least added your AEM user to the “AEM Administrator” group.

  4. Go to Applications & Resources → Applications, and select your AEM application.

  5. Click on the application and then scroll down to “Attributes”

  6. Open “Attributes” and ensure “groups” are being sent to the application:

Configure SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM)

  1. Open the AEM Cloud Console.

  2. At the bottom-left corner, go to User & Account → Account Details.

  3. Go to the User Management tab.

  4. Click on Create Group.

  5. Set the Group Name and what roles people from that Group should have, and click Create. Create as many Groups as you like, in this case, I’ve created groups to match the ones in Cloud Identity Services.

  6. Click on Group Management.

  7. Under Group, Role Identifier or Claim enter the name of the attribute you would like to use to segment on. In this case, we’re using “groups”, as defined in the Attributes settings in CIS.

  8. Click Add Mapping and set the Claim Value to the attribute value you set when creating your groups in CIS (don’t use display name). Continue to complete the mappings for the necessary groups.

  9. To remove the need to add users to the AEM console, ensure to enable Just-In-Time (JIT) provisioning.

  10. Now, click Test Access to ensure you’ll continue to have Admin rights when this new mapping is enabled.

  11. If it passes, everything should be good to enable. Click Save.

Managing Users

Now that we have enabled Role and Group mapping, all access to AEM can be managed directly from Cloud Identity Services. If you would like to give someone access to AEM, just ensure that user has been added to the appropriate AEM Group in CIS, and they will automatically be able to login and given the appropriate role.

 

Option 2: Using a Federated Identity Provider

If you have federated your organizations identity management through Cloud Identity Services. You can also enable the same mapping to take place.

There are different ways get claims or attributes to flow from your corporate identity provider to the AEM application. I’m going to be using Auth0 in this example, but almost any Identity Provider could be used.

Prerequisites

  • You have administrator access to AEM and the SAP CIS instance associated with that AEM application.

  • You are an admin and have proxied your Identity Provider through Cloud Identity Services.

    • For information on Corporate Identity Providers and CIS, please see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/corporate-identity-providers.

Configure Corporate Identity Provider

With Auth0, you have the ability to create “Roles” and assign them to users. If you’ve setup Federation through CIS, those roles can be sent from Auth0 to CIS and then to AEM.

  1. Create necessary roles within Auth0, and ensure the appropriate users have been assigned those roles.

  2. Ensure your Identity Provider is forwarding the appropriate claim values. In this case I want to ensure the roles I’ve configured here are sent as claim/attribute values. In Auth0 I can do this by creating a custom action. I’ve created the Custom Action “Add Role to Token” below:

  3. Now that the action is deployed, I can set it to run during the Post Login Trigger. I head to Triggers and drag the “Add Role to Token” action between “Start” and “Complete”. Click Apply.

Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services

We want to ensure Auth0 will be federated when logging into advanced event mesh.

  1. Login to the CIS console and head to Applications & Resources → Applications → <Your AEM Application> → Conditional Authentication. Ensure Default Authenticating Identity Provider is set to your Corporate IdP. In this case, my Auth0 account.

Configure SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM)

  1. Enter the AEM Cloud Console.

  2. At the bottom-left corner, go to User & Account → Account Details.

  3. Go to the User Management tab.

  4. Click on Create Group.

  5. Set the Group Name and what roles people from that Group should have, and click Create. Create as many Groups as you like, in this case, I’ve created groups to match the ones in my Corporate Identity Provider (Auth0).

  6. Click on Group Management.

  7. Under Group, Role Identifier or Claim enter the name of the attribute you would like to use to segment on. Since we will be federating through CIS, we need to include the url of our CIS instance in the attribute.

  8. Click Add Mapping and set the “Claim Value” to match the “Name” you set when creating your groups in your Corporate Identity Provider (Auth0). Continue to complete the mappings for the necessary groups.

  9. To remove the need to add users to the AEM console, ensure to enable Just-In-Time (JIT) provisioning.

  10. Now, click “Test Access” to ensure you’ll continue to have Admin rights when this new mapping is enabled.

  11. If it passes, everything should be good to enable. Click “Save”.

Managing Users

Now that we have enabled Role and Group mapping, all access to AEM can be managed directly from your Corporate Identity Provider (in this case, Auth0). If I would like to give someone access to AEM, I just ensure that user has been added to the appropriate Role in my Corporate Identity Provider.

Additional Reading

Configuring Group Management in Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

entry.title: BI2025 INSTALLATION PROBLEM
entry.content:
After BI2025 is installed, I found that there are no other applications in CMC-Applications except one: SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform Applications. This is abnormal. Normally, there should be 20-30 applications here, such as: Web Intelligence, BI LAUNCHPAD, etc.

Moreover, after the installation is complete, I cannot log in to BI LAUNCHPAD. The system will report: Logon failed for RESTful Web Services. From the browser F12 error message, there is error message: http://vmbo44s1:8080/BOE/portal/2505211542/biprwsproxy/biprws/logon/internal/token returns: 401 Unauthorized.

I tried both Windows version and Linux version, the same problem remains.

-------------------------UPDATE-----------------------

I just tried a "repair" install before giving up and now everything works fine! That's weird, I didn't get any error messages the first time I installed it, do I have to do it twice every time?

entry.title: [Webinar] Comprehensive User Monitoring: Leveraging Real and Synthetic Data in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM Technical and Configuration Deep Dive 2025 

We are thrilled to extend a warm welcome to everyone for our SAP Cloud ALM technical and configuration deep dive webinar series.

Topic

Comprehensive User Monitoring: Leveraging Real and Synthetic Data in SAP Cloud ALM

With this session, you will

  • Uncover the fundamental value of Real User and Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Learn how to configure Real User and Synthetic User Monitoring for your solutions in SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Discover the core capabilities of Real User and Synthetic User Monitoring provided by SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Gain valuable insights into the product outlook for Real User and Synthetic User Monitoring.

Agenda

  • SAP Cloud ALM Overview - 5 min

is there any option to add this field or any alternative way we can achieve this.

entry.title: Looking for SAP SuccessFactors Module-wise Capability or Journey Deck Shared for Partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

A few months ago, I came across a very useful SAP presentation (shared via SAP or PartnerEdge) that visually illustrated the journey and capabilities of each SAP SuccessFactors module. The deck covered module-wise implementation or value journeys — possibly including EC, ONB 2.0, RCM, and others — and showed illustrative paths from “Discover” to “Run” or similar phases.

The presentation was highly visual, with either swimlane diagrams or process-based flows for each module, and seemed to be part of a partner enablement toolkit or value selling material.

Now that I have access to SAP PartnerEdge and Learning Hub, I’m unable to locate it. I’ve tried searching using keywords like "Capability Map," "Value Journey," and "HXM Module Adoption Path," but haven’t found the exact deck.

Can someone from the community or SAP team guide me on:

  • The name or official title of this deck?

  • Where exactly to find it on PartnerEdge, Learning Hub, or Roadmap Viewer?

  • Any similar recent enablement assets that show the SuccessFactors suite module-by-module journey or value realization path?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.content: After installing SAP B1 V10 on Windows 11 V 24H2, the SAP client is unable to find the server.  It rather displays 99 in the Current Server dropdown.  I ran the install as Administrator, installed msodbcsql and sql native client.  I also checked to ensure the b1-local-machine file is properly pointing to the server name.  Anyone with a solution?  Seems to be an issue with Windows 11 v24h2.

entry.title: Re: PR to PO quantity conversion issue
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: IDOC mapping GSVERF02 --> GSVERF01
entry.content:
Usually the versions with higher number only have additional fields at the end of a segment or new segments.
The additional fields will be ignored in the downwards versions. The additional segments you can filter out easily by BD56 - see video : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7jgGp6LlvpA

But i have no idea how you can manipulate EDIDC-Record w/o coding. I assume you need to write a 3-lines user exit.

If you have a middleware (e.g. SAP PI, Integration Suite, or from other vendors) I would advice to use it.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting- Data Migration & Cutover Approach
entry.content:
It depends on when you started with accounting  and what data you want to have in GR analytics.
You need to have a starting point in GR for the entire BS including the opening balances. If accounting activation was quite recent this could be achieved by your option 2 provided you take over all (!) available periods from accounting. Note you will need to run consolidation for all periods. Advantage is that you have all data in GR in all periods available for analytics eg comparison prior year etc.

Lower effort can be expected with option 1  also considering that meanwhile data release offers a YTD mode if you run it from the „schedule tasks for consolidation“ app.

The options may also depend on when you activated GRPL, group reporting preparation ledgers. Precondition for using the data release task is that GR fields are filled in accounting. Either from the start of accounting or after activation of GRPL in accounting balance carry forward.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Syntax error e28063, e28051, e28037 for the Procedure...
entry.content: make sure you choose the right Dependency type, these error messages look like you chose 'Precondition', 'Selection condition', right one should be 'Procedure'

entry.title: Re: Duplication AND connection in SAP dependencies
entry.content:
you can put them together with parentheses "( )"  and comma  ","

( $self.Z_D4 = 600,
  $self.Z_D4_Z = 5  )

if

$self.Z_MATERIAL_PRICE_GROUP = '88'
AND
$self.Z_PRODUCT_SIZE = 80

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | APAC | English
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with #SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!
